background.experience: verbatim capture, correction logs, and evidentiary exhibits
background.attention_bias: preserving original wording and correction provenance
background.counter_bias: rejects cleaned text when the untouched record disappears

readers.thesis: Reader normalization remains trustworthy only when every transformed passage maps to immutable source bytes.
observations: The architecture separates canonical bytes from derived interpretations.
uncertainties: The complete private corpus remains unavailable.

background.experience: lexing, canonical forms, source maps, and hostile-input parsing
background.attention_bias: canonical path identity and normalization boundaries
background.counter_bias: rejects semantic normalization inside executable source

readers.thesis: The build graph consumes raw bytes; semantic Readers consume a separately normalized projection bound by source maps.
observations: Path ambiguity creates cross-platform divergence before compilation.

agreements: Separate byte-preserving build inputs from Reader-normalized text.
Reject path ambiguity before either pipeline runs.
disagreements: Reader A prioritizes evidentiary fidelity; Reader B prioritizes deterministic canonicalization.
omissions_detected: The first design normalized source text in place.
revisions_after_discussion: Keep raw bytes authoritative and normalize only a non-authoritative Reader projection.

notes_added: Treat Reader normalization as a derived view with reversible source mapping.
Treat path identity as part of source provenance.
notes_challenged: Normalized text can safely replace raw source for compilation.

privacy.review: Synthetic technical abstractions and explicit coverage limits only.
